title: crepis praemorsa subsp. corymbosa
intro: Crepis praemorsa subsp. corymbosa, also known as rough hawk's beard, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Crepis praemorsa subsp. corymbosa include reducing inflammation and relieving digestive issues, as well as supporting immune function and promoting wound healing. Crepis praemorsa subsp. corymbosa can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in herbal infusions and salves to utilize its medicinal properties.
